codeReview.summary: Core user list/add/edit/delete behavior is implemented and broadly covered, but two blocking integrity issues remain in admin user CRUD: invalid group IDs surface as unhandled 500s, and deleting the last admin account is currently allowed.

issues.description: Create/update validation only checks that `userGroup` is numeric and does not verify the group exists before write. Requests like `POST /api/admin/users` or `PUT /api/admin/users/:id` with `userGroup: 999` hit foreign-key errors at insert/update (lines 349-374 and 489-502) and return 500 Internal Server Error instead of a controlled 4xx validation response.

`DELETE /api/admin/users/:id` deletes any existing user without protecting the last admin account. The code has a last-admin demotion guard in PUT, but DELETE lacks the same invariant check, so removing the only group-1 user succeeds and can strand the system without any admin principal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Current user-route tests cover last-admin demotion protection but do not include regression tests for last-admin deletion or invalid-group 500 behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `app/apps/server/src/__tests__/users.test.ts` has `prevents demoting the last admin` and happy-path delete tests, but no case for deleting the only admin account or for create/update with non-existent `userGroup`.

summary: FAIL: user-crud-admin contains two blocking integrity defects (invalid-group 500s and last-admin deletion) that must be fixed before Milestone 4 can pass scrutiny.
